payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.
Comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.
Comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.title: Acting President, Long Island Rail Road

payload.description: Replacing and upgrading the existing Jamaica Substation in Richmond Hill, New York. Substation reached end of useful life. Includes technological upgrades to sustain increased loads from Grand Central Madison and Jamaica Capacity Improvements. Design-Build construction started December 2022, slated for completion October 2025.

Comprehensive ADA station upgrade program at St. Albans station. Phase 1 begins weekends April 6-7, Phase 2 in fall. Upon completion includes station elevator for first time, reconstructed pedestrian tunnel, new HelpPoint emergency kiosks, enhanced security and communication systems, upgrades to lighting, parking, sidewalks, staircases and more.

Replacement of three critical Double Slip switches and a Diamond switch allowing trains to operate from any track in the Park Avenue Tunnel to any platform within Grand Central Terminal. Switches in service since 2000. Crews working around the clock weekends on upper-level Park Avenue Tunnel. Expected to conclude by end of March 2024.

payload.project_name: CP 4, 5, and 6 Signal and Control Upgrades
payload.description: Upgrade of control points CP 4, CP 5, CP 6 overseeing critical junctions where Harlem Line intersects with Hudson Line. Modernizing from fifty-year-old DATATRAN analog system to modern ethernet fiber optic DWDM-based signal and communication equipment with micro-processor-driven control systems. Decentralizing control and implementing autonomous operation for each CP.

Elevating two tracks north of Garrison station to address tidal and storm surges on the Upper Hudson line. Delivering over 400 tons of stone. Depositing 9 to 12 trucks of ballast nightly, achieving elevation increase of 10 to 12 inches for Track 2. Expected to elevate track by 18 inches above original level.
payload.location: north of Garrison station, Upper Hudson line

location_normalized.raw_text: north of Garrison station, Upper Hudson line

payload.project_name: Metro-North Station Dry Line System Installation
payload.description: Installing dry line systems at 25 Metro-North stations with outdated or no existing systems. Complete replacement of dry line infrastructure including removal of outdated equipment, installation of new galvanized high-pressure steel lines and upgraded pipe support system. Stations span New Haven, Harlem, and Hudson lines. Supported by three contracts through MTA Small Business Mentoring Program.

payload.project_name: West Side Yard Slip Switch Replacement
payload.description: State of good repair effort replacing six slip switches in West Side Yard, Manhattan. Total of nine new slip switches installed over past three years. Updated design replaces moveable frog with rigid frog, reducing switch mechanisms from three to two per slip switch.
Largest such margin yet

payload.raw_value_text: Both total AM and PM peaks for the month topped 95%
OTP on weekend trains hit 97% for the month
100% on-time performance on Saturday, March 9, Sunday, March 10, and Sunday, March 17th

payload.description: Purdy's Metro-North Station made fully accessible with opening of new elevator and connecting sidewalk. Third Metro-North station made accessible in 2024.
National Transit Employee Appreciation Day. MTA-wide event at Grand Central. Chairman Janno Lieber and agency heads tried out other jobs as show of appreciation.
MTA Empower Women Event & Awards. Four LIRR women honored for empowering colleagues.
Metro-North participated in Partnership for NYC's Career Discovery Week, welcomed 22 students from Bronx Design and Construction Academy.

First Confidential Close Call Reporting System (C3RS) Symposium hosted by Metro-North. Representatives from Amtrak, LIRR, NJT, Metra, Coaster, SEPTA, Union Pacific Railroad, Volpe Center, FRA, NASA, SLSI, TSI attended.
Metro-North launched fourth locomotive train wrap in Heritage Wrap Series, a tribute to Penn Central Railroad Corporation.
LIRR Safety FOCUS Day. President Rob Free and senior leadership met with Maintenance of Equipment and Engineering Department employees at Morris Park Locomotive Shop.
LIRR and MTA Construction & Development replacing and upgrading Jamaica Substation
Jamaica Substation is an LIRR facility
Metro-North and MTA Construction & Development installing dry line systems
Dry line systems installed at Metro-North stations
West Side Yard slip switch replacement by LIRR Engineering Department
St. Albans ADA station upgrade by LIRR
Diamond crossover replacement program by Metro-North
CP 4, 5, 6 upgrades by Metro-North
Garrison Track Raising by Metro-North Engineering Department

payload.entity_name: Joint Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad Committee
payload.description: Members include: Samuel Chu (Co-Chair), Blanca Lopez (Co-Chair), Gerard Bringmann, Norman Brown, Michael Fleischer, Randolph Glucksman, David Mack, Vinnie Tessitore, Midori Valdivia, Neal Zuckerman

payload.raw_value_text: 34% share for trips to GCM and 66% share to Penn Station on weekdays

payload.claim_text: The OTP of 99.3% was our best February performance in Metro-North's history, as well as only the third time that we reached 99% OTP in any given month.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

payload.claim_text: February's OTP of 96.3% was the best mark the Railroad has posted for the month of February since 2012.
payload.subject: Long Island Rail Road

payload.description: Second-best recovery month since the pandemic

payload.raw_value_text: 33% of the trips arrive at GCM and 67% arrive at Penn Station on weekends
